name: Jishnu Dev Varma
organization: Office of the Governor of Maharashtra
bio: Hon'ble Shri Jishnu Dev Varma is the Governor of Maharashtra and a distinguished public leader from the Manikya dynasty of Tripura. His public life brings together constitutional service, regional leadership, cultural stewardship and a sustained commitment to tribal development, education, health, art and culture.
honours: Governor of Maharashtra; former Governor of Telangana; former Deputy Chief Minister of Tripura; former Convener of INTACH Tripura Chapter.
qualifications: B.A. Honours in English Literature, University of Calcutta.

name: Kailash Satyarthi
designation: Founder, Satyarthi Movement for Global Compassion; Founder, Kailash Satyarthi Children's Foundation; Founder, Bachpan Bachao Andolan; United Nations Secretary-General's SDG Advocate
bio: Shri Kailash Satyarthi is an Indian children's-rights advocate and Nobel Peace Laureate. Born in Vidisha, Madhya Pradesh, he left a career in electrical engineering to campaign against child labour, trafficking and exploitation. Through Bachpan Bachao Andolan, the Global March Against Child Labour and the Satyarthi Movement for Global Compassion, his work has helped free and rehabilitate children while advancing every child's right to education.
honours: Nobel Peace Prize 2014, shared with Malala Yousafzai, for their struggle against the suppression of children and young people and for the right of all children to education; Robert F. Kennedy Human Rights Award; Friedrich Ebert Stiftung Human Rights Award; Wallenberg Medal, University of Michigan; Harvard Humanitarian of the Year; United Nations Secretary-General's SDG Advocate.
qualifications: B.E. Electrical Engineering, Samrat Ashok Technological Institute, Vidisha; M.E. High Voltage Engineering; postgraduate diploma in transformer design; recipient of honorary doctorates from universities in India and overseas.

name: Mohan Munasinghe
designation: Founder Chairman, Munasinghe Institute for Development; Chairman, MIND Group; Chairman, Climate and Conservation Consortium; Honorary Senior Adviser to the Government of Sri Lanka
address: MIND Head Office: 10/1 De Fonseka Place, Colombo 5, Sri Lanka
bio: Professor Mohan Munasinghe is a Sri Lankan physicist, engineer, economist and sustainable-development leader. As Vice-Chair of the Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change during the Fourth Assessment cycle, he shared in the IPCC's 2007 Nobel Peace Prize. He is Founder Chairman of the Munasinghe Institute for Development and is widely recognised for the Sustainomics framework linking economy, environment and society.
honours: Shared in the 2007 Nobel Peace Prize as Vice-Chair of the Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change; Blue Planet Prize Laureate 2021; Officer of the French Legion of Honour; Deshamanya national honour of Sri Lanka; Zayed International Prize for the Environment; Eminence in Engineering Award, Institution of Engineers Sri Lanka; Sarvodaya Award for Humanity, Peace and Development.
qualifications: B.A. (Hons.) Engineering, University of Cambridge; M.A., University of Cambridge; S.M. and Professional Engineer in Electrical Engineering, Massachusetts Institute of Technology; PhD in Solid State Physics, McGill University; M.A. Development Economics, Concordia University; honorary doctorates.

name: Tehemton Burjor Mirza
designation: Vada Dasturji of the Iranshah Atash Behram, Udvada
bio: Vada Dasturji Tehemton Burjor Mirza is one of the high priests of the Iranshah Atash Behram in Udvada, among the most sacred Zoroastrian fire temples in India. Installed on 11 February 2024 by the Udvada Athornan Anjuman and the Udvada Samast Anjuman, he serves alongside Vada Dasturji Khurshed Dastoor and helps carry forward the spiritual stewardship of the Iranshah tradition.
honours: Appointed the sixth high priest of the Iranshah Atash Behram and the second Vada Dasturji in the restored dual high priest arrangement at Udvada. His installation restored a long-standing dual gaddi tradition after an eight-year vacancy and was widely covered by Parsi community publications including Parsi Times, Parsi Khabar and Parsiana.
qualifications: Ordained Navar and Martab at the Iranshah Atash Behram, Udvada; trained at the Dadar Athornan Institute in Mumbai; born into hereditary athornan priestly lineage on both maternal and paternal sides.
website: No personal website. Community coverage at parsi-times.com, parsikhabar.net, parsiana.com
